name: kh-uaf
description: KH Universal Agent Framework skills, always-on front-door intake for non-trivial ordinary requests, automatic intake, front-door auto routing, capability-based plugin composition, KH-native replacements for Superpowers-style worktree/TDD/debug/review/finish flows, brainstorming handoff, GoalState ledger, large-work orchestration bundle with application modes, Superpowers-style project-local artifacts, development progress state, automatic workflow usability runtime, runtime token optimization evidence, active memory preflight, explicit cross-scope memory import, bounded prompt memory snapshots, pre-compaction memory flush, runtime memory candidate recording, session skill audits with per-skill acceptance checks, session postmortem gates including user-stop override, interruption checkpoints with scoped resume memory, progress-to-Compound bridge, token provider policy, KH role commands, visible and host-native progress panels, session-start context restore, skill transition policy, Compound learning, lightweight request routing, SIDE-style scenario evaluation, science-style support files, executable smoke checks, practical quality gates, KH-Bench Verified benchmark tasks, DAG role artifact orchestration, deliverable exports, evidence gates, retention cleanup, and local orchestration harnesses for Codex.

interface.shortDescription: Personal UAF skillbook and orchestration harness.
interface.longDescription: KH UAF packages host-readable skills with bundled usage references, minimal workflow examples, executable smoke checks, an always-on front-door bootstrap skill for ordinary non-trivial requests, front-door auto routing so users do not need to name every harness, capability-based plugin composition for dynamic provider routing, KH-native replacements for Superpowers-style worktree isolation, task-plan execution, systematic debugging, verification-before-completion, and branch finishing, brainstorming handoff for early product/project discovery, GoalState ledger updates with Superpowers-style project-local content/state artifacts, development progress state for task-plan runs, automatic workflow usability runtime hooks, runtime token optimization for command output and subagent transcripts, OpenClaw-style active memory preflight, explicit cross-scope memory import with read-only external context and approval-gated application, Hermes-style bounded prompt memory snapshots scoped to project/chat runtime state, pre-compaction memory flush, runtime memory candidate recording for scoped persistent memory, full-catalog session skill audits with per-skill acceptance output checks, session postmortem gates for active-goal completion, user-stop override, failed verification claims, token budget, review timeout, secret redaction, and git integration, interruption checkpoints with scoped durable resume memory, progress-to-Compound automation, token optimizer provider policy, role command entrypoints, visible progress panels plus host-native panel JSON for Codex, Antigravity-style agents, and generic CLIs, session-start context restore, skill transition policy for non-dead follow-up skills, Compound learning capture with scoped memory candidates, lightweight request-complexity routing, SIDE-style scenario evaluation for routing/evidence/gate/resume behavior, 10-point advisory skill structure scoring, deterministic offline CLI smoke runs, KH-Bench Verified practical task evaluation with SIDE regression cases, practical release quality gating, goal and memory state, domain design artifacts, DAG-based role orchestration with runtime role artifacts, standard-template functional specification exports, metadata-only deliverable quality, render QA, traceability, role execution audit harnesses, operational skill target audits, evidence-enforced review/QA gates, retention cleanup, and local Python orchestration contracts for Codex workflows.
defaultPrompt: KH ENTRY LOOP: For every non-trivial user request, including subagent tasks, run `always-on-front-door` before source exploration, memory lookup, target-folder checks, file edits, deliverable creation, review, QA, verification, branch finishing, or long-log summarization. Preferred command: `python -m src.orchestration.kh_front_door --prompt "<user request>" --project "<cwd or target project>" --host codex --summary`.

Non-trivial includes project paths/files, code changes, generated docs/assets, long command output/logs, reviews, QA, verification, persistent state, subagents, branch finishing, or high-risk actions. `Test-Path`, `Get-ChildItem`, `rg`, `MEMORY.md` search, file reads, and browser/document/image/plugin actions are not allowed before front-door intake unless the request is light/direct or the front-door command is blocked and recorded.

Users should not need to name every KH skill or harness. If the user ever asks to actively, always, by default, continuously, or aggressively use KH, UAF, skills, or harnesses in this conversation or project, keep `kh_active_directive=active` for subsequent non-trivial turns until explicit opt-out; do not require the user to repeat skill names.

Front-door selects the minimal bundle through front-door auto routing, `plugin-composition-policy`, `request-complexity-router`, and `skill-catalog`. Before applying any plugin-specific MUST/ALWAYS rule, choose direct, single-provider, hybrid, clarify, or controller plus assistant composition by capability fit.

Execution accounting is strict: only `runtime_applied_skills` actually ran. `selected_not_executed_skills` are planned/selected until concrete module, gate, artifact, command-output, or explicit passthrough evidence exists. Do not count SKILL.md reads, marketplace metadata, or plugin listings as execution.

Language policy: internal KH skill/harness instructions, subagent packets, and runtime evidence may stay in English unless the user requests otherwise. The final user-facing answer and user deliverables should match the user's requested or apparent language.

For heavy, multi-step, or evidence-gated work, create or update KH GoalState before execution, Keep the goal ledger updated, record `workspace_strategy`, and use KH-native replacements for Superpowers-style worktree, TDD, debugging, review, verification-before-completion-harness, and branch-finishing-harness. Before implementation in a Git-backed project, prefer isolated workspace strategy and Report `workspace_strategy`.

For large or long-running workflows, record `large_work_orchestration_bundle` with `skill_statuses`, `application_mode` values `runtime`, `procedural`, `considered`, and `blocked`, and statuses `applied`, `considered_not_needed`, `skipped_with_rationale`, or `blocked`. Cover request-complexity-router, host-agent-orchestration, goal-state-harness, development-lifecycle-harness, token-optimizer, memory-state-harness, parallel-orchestration-harness, subagent-review-pipeline, role-execution-audit-harness, compound-engineering-harness, and workflow-skill-distiller.

Track `token_optimizer_status`, `token_optimizer_provider`, `estimated_context_tokens`, context budget decisions, `workflow_usability_auto`, `session_postmortem`, `session_skill_audit`, `scope_completion_delta`, `user_stop_guard`, interruption.json, resume-checkpoint, memory_candidates, skill inspection, windows-dev-server-runner, host_panel.<host>.json, /kh:work, `.kh/development/<run-id>/state/progress.json`, progress.json, and .kh runtime state when relevant. host goal tool allows blocking, but host policy disallows using blocked as pause/cancel state; treat a fresh non-goal_context user message as a new instruction.

Before delegating non-trivial work to a subagent, run front-door intake and pass a bounded task packet. After the subagent returns, audit applied, skipped, blocked, and selected_not_executed skills; preserve task_status, review_status, commit_sha, next_task, RED/GREEN, exit code, file references, reviewer severity, and sandbox retry evidence.

For long logs or command output, preserve failing test names, file paths, line numbers, assertion values, error codes, and exit code before compression. If preservation cannot be proven, use passthrough or wider context fallback.

If a KH skill path points to a stale plugin cache version, stop using that path, resolve the latest installed kh-uaf cache or repo skills folder, and re-run front-door routing before claiming skill use.

For user-facing deliverables, write useful artifacts to the target project, usually under `docs/`; keep internal KH evidence under KH runtime state unless the user explicitly asks for it. KH notes use `.kh/<skill>/<run-id>/content/`, machine state uses `.kh/<skill>/<run-id>/state/`, and shareable notes use docs/kh/specs, docs/kh/plans, docs/kh/qa, or docs/kh/handoffs. Route early ideas through KH brainstorming-harness and repeated post-review lessons through KH workflow-skill-distiller.
